These codes are part of a standardized system used to classify and code all diagnoses, symptoms, and procedures recorded in the clinical setting.  There are two major versions of the ICD code used in healthcare:  ICD-9-CM (Clinical Modification) – The previous version used in the United States before the transition to ICD-10. ICD-10-CM – The updated version that is currently in use. The icd_code represents these codes, which are typically structured as a series of numbers or alphanumeric characters. For example:  ICD-9: A code like "250.00" for type 2 diabetes without complications. ICD-10: A code like "E11.9" for type 2 diabetes without complications.
